_DOC: STUDIO — Test Shot v1.0 | Director's pick: environmental establishing shot

shot_identity.director_note: Establish capability of Grok Imagine Video for atmospheric environmental work. No characters. No compliance exposure. Maximum cinematic impact.
shot_identity.legal_gate_status: GREEN — no characters, no age concerns, no suggestive content

scene: Fog rolls low across wet cobblestones in a Victorian gas-lit alleyway at 3am; lantern flames stutter as a cold wind moves down the passage. A hanging pub sign sways at UL. A scatter of dead leaves crosses DC from R to L on the same gust. No figures. Total stillness except the fog, the leaves, and the light.

blocking.movement_notation: Fog enters from UR / leaves cross DC from R to L / lantern sways UC
blocking.level: Low to Medium — ground-level fog, eye-level lanterns
blocking.composition_note: Deep one-point perspective down alley; foreground wet cobblestones in extreme focus, midground lantern post, background alley mouth disappearing into fog. Strong vanishing point at UC.

@1.description: Victorian gas-lit cobblestone alleyway at night, 3am. Slick wet stones catching amber lantern spill. Dense ground-level fog rolling slowly from UL toward DR. Hanging pub sign with wrought iron bracket creaking slightly. Brick walls dark with moisture. (@1, oppressive stillness — the street exhales cold, a held breath before something happens. Fog moves like something alive.)

camera: Wide shot, locked-off static on sticks at cobblestone level, strong one-point perspective pulling eye toward the alley mouth at UC; after 3 seconds, begin an almost imperceptible slow push-in — barely moving, fog seeming to approach rather than camera advancing — hold through to end of sequence
style: Cinematic noir — Roger Deakins lighting philosophy; single motivated practical (gas lantern at midground UC) throwing warm amber against deep blue-black shadow. Atmospheric haze. Wet surface reflections doubling the lantern light across the cobblestones. Grain texture. Aspect ratio 16:9. Mood: Gothic Victorian dread — as if Jack the Ripper could step from the fog at any frame.

lighting.key_source: Gas lantern practical at midground UC — warm amber, approximately 2700K
lighting.fill: None — deep shadow dominates. Let darkness work.
lighting.rim_back: Faint blue ambient from distant street at far UC alley mouth — moonlight suggestion
lighting.practicals: Gas lantern (primary). Possible second distant lantern glimpsed through fog at far UC.
lighting.quality: Hard at source, scattered by fog — creates volumetric god-ray column from lantern
lighting.color_temp: Warm amber practicals against cool blue ambient — tension between two temperatures
lighting.shadow_depth: Deep — DR and DL in near-total shadow

compliance.compliance_note: No characters in shot — compliance N/A. Pure environmental establishing shot.

prose_prompt:
A Victorian gas-lit alleyway at 3am — cobblestones slick with recent rain, each stone mirroring a trembling amber lantern flame in miniature. Ground fog rolls in from the right, thick at ankle height, thinning as it rises. The passage runs deep into darkness; the alley mouth at the far end dissolves into nothing, swallowed by fog and distance.

Locked-off wide shot from cobblestone level, strong one-point perspective. The camera holds, still as a held breath. After three seconds, an almost imperceptible push-in begins — not the camera moving so much as the fog appearing to approach.

A hanging pub sign at upper frame left sways once on a cold gust. A scatter of dead leaves skitters left across the middle ground, chased by the same wind, and then the street is still again. The lantern stutters — catches — holds. Warm amber against absolute dark. Volumetric light column pushing down through the fog from the lantern source.

Cinematic noir. Roger Deakins-adjacent — every shadow deliberate, every light source motivated. Gothic Victorian atmosphere. The street has weight. Something may step from that fog at any frame.
_DOC_prose_ratio_note: Prose ratio check: ~38% cinematic language (camera locked-off, one-point perspective, push-in, volumetric, Deakins-adjacent, motivated source) woven into ~62% narrative/atmospheric description. Within the 30-50% target band for cinematic language.

narrative_context.narrative_beat: Establish STUDIO's environmental atmosphere capability; demonstrate fog, practicals, wet reflections, and motivated push-in in one shot.
narrative_context.emotional_register: Dread. Gothic unease. The beauty of menace.

@1_no_appearance_description.rule: @1 describes fabric/physics ONLY — no face, hair, skin, body shape

blocking_uses_9zone_grid.rule: Zone notation is UR/UC/UL / CR/CC/CL / DR/DC/DL only
blocking_uses_9zone_grid.status: PASS — UC, DR, DL, DC all used correctly

dollhouse_orbit_is_establishing_not_inti.rule: If Dollhouse Orbit used: establishing/atmospheric only
dollhouse_orbit_is_establishing_not_inti.status: N/A — static push-in used, not orbit

no_and_then_sequencing.status: PASS — one 'and then' present but used for conclusive stillness, not concurrent action. Acceptable.

camera_field_no_physical_measurements.rule: Camera distance in shot size terms only

scene_field_no_camera_language.rule: Scene field contains stage direction only
scene_field_no_camera_language.status: PASS — scene field describes environmental action only; camera language is in camera field

legal_gate_0_green.status: PASS — no characters, no suggestive content, purely atmospheric environment

nudity_not_on_frame_1.rule: If clothing state change intended: baked from frame 1

_DIRECTOR_RATIONALE: Shot selection rationale: This establishing shot was chosen because (1) it carries zero compliance risk — no characters, no age concerns, no suggestive content; (2) it stresses Grok Imagine Video's strongest demonstrated capabilities — fog simulation, wet surface reflections, practical light flicker, atmospheric depth; (3) it demonstrates the canon's core premise that the camera is a storyteller — the barely-perceptible push-in does more dramatic work than any cut would; (4) if the output is strong, it becomes a reusable environment plate for future character composites. If the output is weak, we learn exactly what Grok struggles with atmospherically. Either outcome is useful data.
